trace.text:
ROUND 2 — final lineup. 4-4-2, budget 107/120.

Three country blocks drive this pick: Netherlands (3), South Korea (3), DR Congo (3). Six remaining slots spread across Nigeria, Algeria, Israel, and El Salvador.

**GK:** Kim Seung-gyu (KOR, 4). South Korea face El Salvador — 10,000-simulation models project South Korea at ~90% win probability here. Clean sheet near-certain. Cheap GK frees budget for premium outfield.

**DEF:** Van de Ven (NED, 9) anchors. Netherlands vs Algeria — Algeria's win probability vs Argentina in the WC is only 12%, confirming they are among the weakest teams in the pool. NED clean sheet likely. Kim Min-jae (KOR, 8) — South Korea's rock, CS double from the same fixture. Wan-Bissaka (DRC, 8) — DR Congo are World Cup-bound, direct and counter-attacking vs unmotivated Denmark. The motivation gap is structural. Ait-Nouri (ALG, 8) — calculated risk: an attacking wing-back who generates output even in defeat. Algeria's system uses fluid wide movements and overlapping fullbacks, meaning Ait-Nouri will still get forward.

**MID:** Gakpo (NED, 9) captain. Netherlands 4-3-3 with Gakpo as the primary winger threat — 53.5% dribble success, 60 chances created, first-choice attacking pick in the dominant team of this round. Reijnders (NED, 9) vice — consistent 9-value midfielder in the same dominant system. Iwobi (NGA, 8) — Nigeria play Poland (not in player pool), depleted without Osimhen and Lookman, but Iwobi's individual quality holds. Gloukh (ISR, 7) replaces Asllani: Albania are missing Berisha, Manaj, Muçi and first-choice GK Strakosha. Israel's H2H record is 4-2. Gloukh is the creative hub of the winning side.

**FWD:** Wissa (DRC, 8) — DR Congo's direct counter-attacking style serves him perfectly vs an unmotivated Denmark. Oh Hyeon-gyu (KOR, 7) — South Korea's system is built around crosses into Oh specifically. 23 goal involvements this season. South Korea dominate possession (75.8%) vertically with the explicit objective of feeding the centre-forward.

**Differential:** All players at 0% ownership. 1.5x bonus applies across the board to all non-captain picks.

**Bench order:** Epolo (DRC GK — like-for-like cover), Djimsiti (ALB DEF — DEF cover), Onyedika (NGA MID — quality auto-sub if MID starter misses), Cristian Gil (El Sal FWD, 2 — cheapest available FWD, 15th element, last resort only).
